message:
fix: security — restrict cliCommand/baseUrl/ollamaBaseUrl from repo configs

Repo-level .cass/config.json should not be able to redirect LLM calls
to arbitrary endpoints. The existing restriction list blocked apiKey,
cassPath, diaryDir, crossAgent, remoteCass, budget, and sanitization,
but missed three fields that control WHERE calls are sent:

- cliCommand: repo could redirect CLI calls to an arbitrary executable
  that receives the full LLM prompt (including code/session content)
- baseUrl: repo could redirect API calls to an exfiltration proxy while
  the user's API key (from env) is sent in the Authorization header
- ollamaBaseUrl: same concern for Ollama endpoints

Also: added FORCE_COLOR=0 to CLI subprocess env (some tools ignore
NO_COLOR when FORCE_COLOR is set), and added defensive .catch() on
the orphaned Promise.all in the timeout race pattern to prevent
unhandled rejections.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
